summary: Summary: The call opened with Speaker2 in a neutral tone while Speaker1 reported repeated disconnects and expressed frustration about having to contact multiple departments (Speaker1: frustration; Speaker2: neutral). Speaker2 calmly walked Speaker1 through diagnostics, asking them to run tests and check whether the DSL cable was being pinched or routed through doorways (Speaker2: neutral). As troubleshooting proceeded, Speaker1 showed frustration and occasional surprise when the issue recurred and fixes were attempted (Speaker1: frustration, surprise). Speaker2 then reset network settings, suggested the customer’s IP may have been lost, and offered their cell number in case the problem returned (Speaker2: neutral). After the IP reset, Speaker1 reacted with surprise, then happiness and felt excited about a likely permanent solution (Speaker1: surprise, happiness, excited). Speaker2 affirmed the importance of customer satisfaction and shifted from neutral to share in the customer’s happiness (Speaker2: neutral, then happiness), and the conversation ended on a positive note with both parties expressing happiness.
